FT_IC1.evidence: ABCD Study Release 5.1, baseline ages 9-10, 21 U.S. sites. States: 'We utilized data from ABCD Study® Release 5.1 including baseline (Y0; ages 9-10) neuroimaging and two-year follow-up (Y2; ages 11-12) addictive screen use survey data.'

FT_IC5.evidence: Original longitudinal neuroimaging analysis with mixed-effects regressions predicting addictive screen use from baseline brain maturation and reward activation measures. Reports inferential statistics and mediation pathways.

FT_IC6.evidence: Published peer-reviewed journal preprint in Biological Psychiatry: Cognitive Neuroscience and Neuroimaging. No duplication identified.

FT_IC2.evidence: Measures three separate qualifying SM/phone domains: addictive videogaming, addictive social media use, addictive phone use. Each paired with individual-trait-level neural outcomes (cortical connectomic maturation/AFC, NAc reward activation). Methods state: 'addictive videogaming, addictive social media use, and addictive phone use questionnaires' administered at Y2.

FT_IC3.evidence: Reports numeric results for SM-outcome relationships. Examples: cross-domain ASU with AFC: β_M1 = −.056, CI [−.083, −.027], p < .001; addictive videogaming: β_M1 = −.060, CI [−.087, −.031], p < .001; addictive phone use: β_M1 = −.039, CI [−.069, −.008], p = .035.

FT_IC4.evidence: Regression coefficients and confidence intervals reported. Also reports negative binomial regression odds ratios for videogaming (OR = 0.97) and indirect-effect estimates through mediation pathways.

FT_EC7_reason: Longitudinal design (Y0→Y2). SM/phone variables (addictive videogaming, social media, phone use) are outcome variables (DV) in all analyses. No analysis anywhere tests SM/phone as IV. Three conditions for EC7 all met: design is longitudinal, SM is DV only, no SM-as-IV.

FT_IC1.evidence: Explicitly states: 'from the Adolescent Brain Cognitive Development Study in the U.S.' and 'Participants were 6,467 youth (39.2% female) who reported playing digital games, from the Adolescent Brain Cognitive Development Study in the U.S.'

FT_IC5.evidence: Original empirical cohort study with prospective analyses. Reports associations between problematic gaming at age 12 and psychotic-like experiences at age 13, moderated by environmental factors.

FT_IC6.evidence: Published peer-reviewed journal article in Journal of Behavioral Addictions. No duplication identified.

FT_IC2.evidence: Study examines 'problematic gaming' only, which is not a qualifying SM/phone modality per criteria §2.4. The paper measures video game addiction and gaming disorder without isolating or separately measuring smartphone or social media use as distinct IV or DV. Gaming-only constructs (without SM/phone isolation) do not qualify. No separately-measured SM or phone variable exists to pair with PLEs.

notes: EXCLUDE under FT-EC2 (no qualifying smartphone/SM variable).

FT_IC1.evidence: Explicitly analyzes ABCD Study data: '2294 participants from the Adolescent Brain Cognitive Development study (Mage = 10.03, 50.6% female) who had resting-state functional magnetic resonance imaging (fMRI) data at baseline and PUSM scores at the four-year follow-up.'

FT_IC5.evidence: Original empirical analysis using connectome-based predictive modeling (CPM), a machine-learning approach applying inferential analysis to whole-brain functional connectivity data to predict PUSM severity.

FT_IC6.evidence: Published peer-reviewed journal article in NeuroImage. No duplication identified.

FT_IC2.evidence: Measures problematic use of social media (PUSM) using the 6-item Social Media Addiction Questionnaire (SMAQ). PUSM is paired with individual-trait-level neural outcome: baseline resting-state fMRI connectivity patterns as predictors. Methods state: 'participants who reported having at least one social media account were prompted to complete the SMAQ.'

FT_IC3.evidence: Reports numeric results for PUSM prediction from neural connectivity. CPM successfully predicted PUSM severity: combined positive and negative networks: r = 0.055, p = 0.016 (controlled model). Permutation testing confirmed significance: p = 0.02.

FT_IC4.evidence: Pearson r correlation coefficients reported: r = 0.055 (controlled model with covariates), p = 0.016. Uncontrolled model: r = 0.066, p = 0.003. Permutation testing with 1000 permutations conducted.

FT_EC7_reason: Longitudinal design (baseline Y0 rs-fMRI predicts Y4 PUSM). PUSM (problematic use of social media) is the DV throughout—predicted from baseline neural connectivity. No analysis anywhere treats PUSM as IV. All three EC7 conditions met.
